attractions.name: 715 Restaurant
attractions.description: 715 is an award-winning, locally owned European-style neighborhood restaurant and bar located in downtown Lawrence. It is celebrated for its simple yet inspired handmade food, including charcuterie, house-made pastas, and steaks, complemented by an extensive wine list and inventive cocktails. Candace Lately, 715mass.com
attractions.hours: Sun-Wed: 3:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Thu-Sat: 3:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Happy Hour: 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM daily. 715mass.com
highlights: European-style neighborhood restaurant and bar located in historic downtown Lawrence. Globalphile
Renowned for handmade pasta, charcuterie, and locally sourced steaks. KCUR
Features an extensive wine list and a creative menu of inventive cocktails. City Lifestyle
Award-winning dining experience known for its rotating seasonal menu and local ingredients. Explore Lawrence
Sourced from surrounding local farms, butchers, and cheesemongers. KCUR
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended as it is a popular destination for fine dining. Be sure to try the chorizo date starter and penne ragu, which are local favorites, and take advantage of the daily happy hour for drink specials. KCUR, Find Me Gluten Free
attractions.nearby: Eldridge Hotel, Mass St. Fish House & Raw Bar, Merchants Pub & Plate, and the various shops and galleries of the Historic Downtown Lawrence district. Globalphile, Explore Lawrence

attractions.name: Free State Brewing Company Free State Brewing Company
attractions.category: Brewery, Restaurant Free State Brewing Company, Wikipedia
attractions.description: Opened in 1989, Free State Brewing Company was the first legal brewery in Kansas in over 100 years. It is located in a renovated historic bus depot in downtown Lawrence and has become a beloved local institution known for its award-winning craft beers and hearty, diverse menu. Free State Brewing Company, Explore Lawrence
attractions.address: 636 Massachusetts Street Free State Brewing Company
attractions.hours: Monday – Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Thursday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Bar Service until 10:00 PM); Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM Free State Brewing Company, Tripadvisor
highlights: First legal brewery in Kansas since 1881 Wikipedia
Housed in a historic former bus depot on Massachusetts Street Explore Lawrence
Flagship beers include the iconic Ad Astra Amber Ale and Copperhead Pale Ale Free State Brewing Company
Features a popular outdoor patio and a two-story glass-fronted dining area Kansas Sampler
Mascot is the abolitionist John Brown, reflecting the area's 'Free State' heritage Wikipedia
Regularly features seasonal favorites like the Garden Party cucumber ale Explore Lawrence
attractions.tips: Arrive early on University of Kansas game days as the brewery is a major local hub and fills up quickly. Look for the historical marker at the entrance detailing John Brown and the Siege of Lawrence. Check the 'What's on Tap' board for unique, limited-run seasonal releases. Explore Lawrence, The Civil War Muse
attractions.nearby: Watkins Museum of History, Liberty Hall, The Granada, South Park Globalphile, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Free State Festival
attractions.description: The Free State Festival celebrates the free spirit of the state of Kansas with films that challenge, inspire, and entertain. This signature annual event brings independent films, live music, comedy, and art experiences to venues across the vibrant college town of Lawrence. (Free State Festival)
attractions.hours: Event times vary daily; typically starting between 12:00 PM and 8:30 PM from June 22 to June 28, 2026 (Free State Festival)
attractions.admission: All Access Festival Pass: $130 (Early Bird through May 31), $150 (Starting June 1); Individual film screenings: $12 - $15; Special events: $25; Select screenings: Free (Free State Festival)
highlights: Independent film screenings that challenge and inspire (Free State Festival)
Live music performances by artists like Robyn Hitchcock (Free State Festival)
Comedy shows and art installations throughout Lawrence (Free State Festival)
Culinary pairings with short films and pizza parties (Free State Festival)
Partnership events with the Lawrence Public Library (Free State Festival)
attractions.tips: Purchase an All Access Pass for priority admission and arrive at least 30 minutes before showtime to guarantee your seat; passes can be picked up at the Lawrence Arts Center front desk beginning June 22 at 12 PM (Free State Festival).
attractions.nearby: Lawrence Arts Center, Liberty Hall, Watkins Museum of History, Massachusetts Street shopping, University of Kansas campus (Globalphile)

attractions.name: Genovese Italian Restaurant
attractions.description: Genovese is a locally owned and chef-driven restaurant specializing in seasonal Italian cuisine located in the heart of historic downtown Lawrence. The kitchen focuses on handcrafted dishes made from scratch using fresh ingredients, offering a menu that blends traditional flavors with inventive culinary techniques (Genovese Official Site).
attractions.hours: Monday-Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM (Genovese Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free entry; menu prices vary (Genovese Menu)
highlights: Handcrafted house-made pastas including spinach fettuccine and pappardelle (Genovese Menu)
Seasonal Italian menu with ingredients prepared fresh from scratch daily (Genovese Official Site)
Popular signature appetizers like crispy zucchini chips and tender grilled octopus (The Pitch)
Extensive wine list and specialized cocktails with daily happy hour specials (Genovese Menu)
Weekend brunch service featuring unique Italian-inspired breakfast dishes (Genovese Official Site)
Housed in a charming historic building with an "old world" atmosphere on Massachusetts Street (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: Start your meal with the highly recommended zucchini chips or the non-rubbery grilled octopus; be sure to check their Facebook page for nightly chef specials, and note that the "small" pasta portions are often generous enough for a full meal (The Pitch, Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Watkins Museum of History, Free State Brewing Company, Eldridge Hotel, Massachusetts Street Shopping District (Globalphile)

attractions.name: Henry's Upstairs
attractions.description: Henry's Upstairs is a funky, low-lit institution in downtown Lawrence known for its eclectic atmosphere and inclusive environment. The venue serves as a cozy coffee house by day (Grounded Coffee) and a vibrant cocktail bar by night, featuring sidewalk seating, periodic art shows, and a rotating schedule of community events. (Explore Lawrence, The Lawrence Times)
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 5:00 PM – 2:00 AM; Wednesday: 5:00 PM – 2:00 AM; Thursday: 5:00 PM – 2:00 AM; Friday: 5:00 PM – 2:00 AM; Saturday: 5:00 PM – 2:00 AM; Sunday: 2:00 PM – 12:00 AM; Monday: Closed (Gaybnb Travel)
attractions.admission: Free (General entry); Event-specific cover charges (e.g., $5 for drag shows and dance parties) (Instagram)
attractions.phone: N/A (Contact via social media or email at henryscoffeeandbar@gmail.com) (Explore Lawrence)
highlights: Funky, low-lit atmosphere with a 'hidden gem' feel in the heart of downtown.
Inclusive, LGBTQ+-friendly space that hosts regular 'Gay Bar' themed nights and events.
Weekly programming including Bimbo Bingo, trivia nights, and drag show competitions.
Signature creative cocktails and a popular espresso martini menu.
Sidewalk seating that offers some of the best people-watching in the Lawrence area.
Sunday viewing parties for popular television series with themed drink specials.
attractions.tips: Ask the bartender about 'gay ice water' and check their Instagram for the latest event covers and drink specials. The upstairs bar can get crowded during themed events, so arrive early to secure a table. (Explore Lawrence, Instagram)
attractions.nearby: The Sandbar, John Brown’s Underground, Massachusetts Street Shopping, Watkins Museum of History, Liberty Hall (Globalphile)

attractions.name: Jefferson's Restaurant and Bar
attractions.description: Jefferson's is a sports-themed, family-friendly restaurant specializing in fresh, never frozen hot wings with 20 Signature Sauces, hand-pattied burgers, and fresh gulf oysters. A staple of the Lawrence community since opening in 1991, it is known for its lively college-town atmosphere and unique decor featuring dollar bills signed by guests.
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
highlights: Famous for "World Famous Wings" with 20 signature sauces and "Double Dipped" options.
Freshly shucked gulf oysters served raw, baked, or with specialty toppings like Oyster Rockefeller.
Lively atmosphere with walls covered in signed dollar bills from patrons.
Extensive menu including hand-pattied burgers, fried pickles, corn nuggets, and loaded mac 'n' cheese.
Daily specials like "Kids Eat Free" on Mondays and 75-cent boneless wings on Tuesdays.
Family-friendly environment with a dedicated kids' menu and student-specific discounts.
attractions.tips: Don't miss the fried pickles, which are a house specialty. For a unique experience, bring a dollar bill and a marker to sign and staple to the wall. The "Double Dipped" wings are highly recommended for those who love extra flavor and a crisp finish. If visiting on a Thursday, take advantage of student specials with a valid ID.
attractions.nearby: Watkins Museum of History, Free State Brewing Co, Liberty Hall, Lawrence Arts Center, Eldridge Hotel, The Burger Stand at the Casbah, University of Kansas Campus.

attractions.name: Ladybird Diner
attractions.description: Ladybird Diner is a quirky, retro-inspired eatery in the heart of downtown Lawrence known for its modern comfort food and exceptional homemade pies. Beyond its culinary acclaim, the diner is celebrated for its strong community spirit and activism, notably its efforts to provide free meals to those in need during the pandemic.
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 8:00 am - 3:00 pm; Thursday: 8:00 am - 3:00 pm; Friday: 8:00 am - 8:00 pm; Saturday: 8:00 am - 3:00 pm; Sunday: 8:00 am - 3:00 pm
highlights: Famous for award-winning, freshly baked pies in varieties like Coconut Cream, Key Lime, and Triple Berry.
Serves elevated diner classics including legendary Chicken Fried Steak, Huevos Rancheros, and Buttermilk-Fried Chicken.
Features Friday night "Blue Plate" specials and a selection of breakfast cocktails.
Highly regarded for its activist mission and deep commitment to local community support.
Offers a vibrant, "no-B.S." atmosphere with a mix of indoor seating and a dog-friendly patio.
attractions.tips: This popular spot does not take reservations and often has a wait, especially on weekends and game days, so arrive early. Pie for breakfast is a local tradition and highly recommended. If you order the Chicken Fried Steak, consider the fried potatoes over cheesy grits if you prefer a crunchier texture. Check their social media for updates on daily specials and Friday night dinner offerings.
attractions.nearby: The Raven Book Store, Watkins History Museum, Liberty Hall, Free State Brewing Co, South Park

attractions.name: Lawrence Arts Center
attractions.description: Founded in 1975, the Lawrence Arts Center is a multidisciplinary community arts organization that serves as the central hub for the local art scene. It offers contemporary art galleries, a 300-seat theater, and a wide variety of classes in visual and performing arts for all ages (Lawrence Arts Center, Tripadvisor).
attractions.hours: Monday – Thursday: 9am – 8pm; Friday: 9am – 9pm; Saturday: 9am – 8pm; Sunday: CLOSED (Lawrence Arts Center)
attractions.admission: Exhibitions: Free; Performances: Varies (e.g., $12 Members, $15 General Admission) (Lawrence Arts Center, Lawrence Arts Center)
highlights: Contemporary art galleries with museum-quality exhibits (Tripadvisor)
Annual Free State Festival featuring film, art, and music (Explore Lawrence)
Extensive arts education programs including visual arts, dance, and theater (Lawrence Arts Center)
Summer Youth Theatre and Lawrence Ballet Theatre performances (Lawrence Arts Center)
Artist-in-Residence programs for emerging artists (Lawrence Arts Center)
Community events like the Souper Bowl Saturday Fundraiser (Lawrence Arts Center)
attractions.tips: Galleries are free and open to the public all year round; consider becoming a member for early class enrollment and ticket discounts (Lawrence Arts Center, Lawrence Arts Center).
attractions.nearby: Lawrence Farmers' Market, The Granada, Watkins Museum of History, Liberty Hall (Globalphile, Explore Lawrence)

attractions.name: Lawrence Busker Festival
attractions.description: The Lawrence Busker Festival is an annual celebration of street performance that transforms downtown Lawrence into a stage for world-class entertainers. For over 18 years, the festival has featured a quirky and exciting mix of magicians, jugglers, acrobats, and fire dancers from around the globe. It is a family-friendly event that captures the unique, 'get weird' spirit of the Lawrence community.
attractions.address: 408 Indiana Street (Mailing/Office), Downtown Lawrence (Event Venue - 800 & 900 blocks of Massachusetts and New Hampshire Streets)
attractions.hours: Thursday: 7:00 PM - 9:00 PM (Movie event); Friday: 5:00 PM - 11:00 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM - 11:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 7:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Donations and tips for performers are encouraged)
highlights: World-class street performers including magicians, jugglers, and acrobats
The Art Tougeau Parade featuring creative art cars and sculptures on wheels
Multiple performance 'pitches' located throughout downtown Lawrence
Local food vendors and craft brews from sponsors like Free State Brewery
Interactive family-friendly entertainment and workshops
Evening fire dancing and high-energy vaudeville acts
attractions.tips: Bring small bills ($1, $5, $10) to tip the street performers as that is their primary source of income. Dress for the weather and bring a lawn chair or blanket for comfortable viewing at the various stages. Parking is available in downtown garages and lots, though it can get crowded during the Art Tougeau Parade on Saturday. Most vendors accept credit cards, but cash is best for tips.
attractions.nearby: Lawrence Arts Center, Free State Brewing Co., Liberty Hall, Watkins Museum of History, University of Kansas Campus, South Park, The Granada

attractions.name: Lawrence Farmers Market (Lawrence Farmers Market)
attractions.description: The Lawrence Farmers Market is a vibrant, producer-only market in downtown Lawrence, Kansas, that has been growing food and community since 1976. It features over 75 vendors offering seasonal produce, meats, baked goods, and artisanal crafts sourced from within a 50-mile radius. (Lawrence Farmers Market, Explore Lawrence)
attractions.address: 824 New Hampshire Street, Lawrence, KS 66044 (Lawrence Farmers Market)
attractions.hours: Saturdays (April 11 – November 21, 2026): 7:30 a.m. – 11:30 a.m.; Holiday Market (Dec 13, 2025): 9:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m. (Lawrence Farmers Market)
highlights: Producer-only market featuring goods from within 50 miles of Lawrence (Lawrence Farmers Market)
Wide selection of seasonal fresh produce, meats, cheese, eggs, and milk (Lawrence Farmers Market)
Variety of artisanal crafts, hot prepared foods, and drinks (Lawrence Farmers Market)
Weekly live music and entertainment by local buskers (Lawrence Farmers Market)
Educational programs and activities specifically for kids (Lawrence Farmers Market)
Accepts SNAP benefits and offers 'Double Up' tokens for fresh foods (Lawrence Farmers Market)
attractions.tips: Arrive early for the best selection of popular items. Free 2-hour parking is available in the lots and garages across New Hampshire Street at 7th and 9th Streets. The market is also easily accessible via city bus routes 1, 6, 7, and 11. (Lawrence Farmers Market, Explore Lawrence)
attractions.nearby: Watkins Museum of History, Lawrence Arts Center, Massachusetts Street, Lawrence Public Library, South Park (Globalphile, KU CLACS)

attractions.name: Liberty Hall
attractions.description: Liberty Hall is a locally owned and operated historic theater in downtown Lawrence, Kansas, featuring a concert hall, independent cinema, and video rental library. Originally erected in 1912 after a series of fires, it serves as a community hub for first-run independent, foreign, and art house films alongside eclectic live performances. (Official Website, Greenbush)
attractions.hours: Wed - Thurs: 3:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Fri - Sun: 1:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Mon - Tue: Closed (Official Website)
highlights: Historic Beaux-Arts style building with an Imperial Roman facade. (Official Website)
Two screens showing first-run independent, foreign, and art house films. (Greenbush)
Eclectic live event history including artists like William Burroughs and Eddie Vedder. (Official Website)
Attached video rental library with an enormous collection of DVD, VHS, and Blu-ray titles. (Greenbush)
Home to La Prima Tazza, a classic downtown coffee house. (Globalphile)
attractions.tips: Parking in downtown Lawrence is metered and enforced from 9:30 AM to 6:00 PM Monday through Saturday. The cinema offers a concession stand that includes beer, wine, and cocktails. (Official Website FAQ, Greenbush)
attractions.nearby: Free State Brewing Company, Eldridge Hotel, Watkins Museum of History, and many local boutiques on Massachusetts Street. (Globalphile)

attractions.name: Love Garden Sounds
attractions.description: Love Garden Sounds is a premier independent record store in Lawrence, Kansas, specializing in a vast selection of new and used vinyl, CDs, and magnetic tapes. Known for its welcoming atmosphere and resident cats, it serves as a cornerstone of the local music community through its robust buy-sell-trade program.
attractions.hours: Monday-Saturday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM
highlights: Extensive collection of rare and vintage cassette tapes and vinyl records
Active buy-sell-trade program for physical music media and stereo equipment
Famous resident shop cats that greet visitors
Voted as one of the best record stores in the United States by various music publications
Central location in the historic downtown Lawrence shopping district
Frequent host to local music scene events and artist releases
attractions.tips: Check their social media for new arrivals as rare tapes and records sell quickly. Bring in your own collection for trade-in credit to get the best deals on new purchases. Don't forget to pet the resident cats while you browse!
attractions.nearby: The Bottleneck, Watkins Museum of History, Lawrence Arts Center, South Park

attractions.name: Lucia Beer Garden + Grill
attractions.description: Lucia Beer Garden + Grill is a gathering space, live music venue, and bar located in historic Downtown Lawrence. It features an extensive craft beer selection, a large garden patio, and two stages showcasing local and regional music. The in-house kitchen serves freshly prepared food from Lawrence, KS Wing Co. Lucia Beer Garden + Grill, Lawrence Chamber of Commerce
attractions.hours: Monday: 4:00 PM - Late; Tuesday: 4:00 PM - Late; Wednesday: 4:00 PM - Late; Thursday: 4:00 PM - Late; Friday: 4:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Saturday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - Late Lucia Beer Garden + Grill
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday Lucia Beer Garden + Grill
attractions.admission: Free for general entry; cover charges may apply for specific ticketed events and live music performances. Vivid Seats, Lucia Beer Garden + Grill
highlights: 700 sq ft garden patio with a private bar Lucia Beer Garden + Grill
Two stages hosting local and regional musicians Lawrence Chamber of Commerce
Weekly Stormy Tuesday Blues Jam Lucia Beer Garden + Grill
Monthly Lucia Acoustic Sessions showcase Lucia Beer Garden + Grill
Extensive craft beer and cocktail selection Lawrence Chamber of Commerce
In-house kitchen featuring Lawrence, KS Wing Co. Explore Lawrence

attractions.name: Massachusetts Street (Mass Street)
attractions.description: Massachusetts Street, commonly known as Mass Street, is the historic main street and central business district of downtown Lawrence, Kansas. It is recognized as a premier destination in the region, featuring a walkable landscape filled with independent shops, galleries, and diverse dining options. Much of the downtown section is listed on the National Register of Historic Places, preserving its mid-19th to early-20th-century architecture (Wikipedia, Downtown Lawrence).
attractions.address: Along Massachusetts between Sixth and South Park Streets, Lawrence, KS 66044
attractions.hours: Shops: Typically Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sun 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Dining/Entertainment: Late night daily (Tripadvisor)
highlights: A walkable, historic downtown district with over 100 independent businesses (Explore Lawrence)
Home to the Free State Brewing Company, the first legal brewery in Kansas in over 100 years (Wikipedia)
Historic landmarks including the Eldridge Hotel and Watkins Museum of History (Wikipedia)
Popular music and entertainment venues like Liberty Hall and the Granada (Globalphile)
Annual events such as the Massachusetts Street Sidewalk Sale and Lawrence Busker Festival (Simple Wikipedia, Downtown Lawrence)
Street musicians and outdoor art installations featured throughout the corridor (Explore Lawrence)
attractions.tips: Metered parking is enforced Mon-Sat from 9:30 AM to 6:00 PM but is free on Sundays and holidays. For longer visits, look for free 2-hour lots on New Hampshire and Vermont streets or park south of the downtown area for a pleasant walk (City of Lawrence, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: South Park, University of Kansas Campus, Kansas River, Lawrence Arts Center (Wikipedia, Explore Lawrence)

attractions.name: Merchants Pub & Plate
attractions.description: Merchants Pub & Plate is a local gastropub located in a historic 1930s bank building in downtown Lawrence, specializing in seasonal, locally-sourced farmhouse fare. The restaurant offers a casual 'come as you are' atmosphere with an eclectic menu designed to be highly responsive to dietary restrictions, including extensive vegan and gluten-free options. Owners Chef TK and Emily Peterson pair their reimaged familiar dishes with a bar program featuring 30 craft beers on tap and hand-crafted cocktails.
attractions.hours: Monday: 3:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Tuesday - Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 9:30 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM
highlights: Located in the historic Merchants National Bank building built in 1930
Thirty craft beers on tap and a selection of seasonal hand-crafted cocktails
Highly accommodating to dietary restrictions with specific menu codes for vegan, gluten-free, and celiac needs
Features unique elements like bathrooms located inside the former bank's vault
Outdoor patio seating available on Massachusetts Street
Daily Happy Hour from 3:00 PM to 5:00 PM with drink and appetizer specials
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for Sunday brunch as it is a popular local destination. Don't miss the chance to visit the restrooms, which are uniquely situated inside the building's original historic bank vault. For sides, the yam fries are a consistent guest favorite, and the restaurant is known for its free bacon addition to the Mac & Cheesemonger.
attractions.nearby: The Burger Stand at the Casbah, Wheatfields Bakery, Free State Brewing Co, Breakout Lawrence, Phoenix Gallery, Hookah House, The Sandbar

attractions.name: Replay Lounge
attractions.description: The Replay Lounge is a legendary rock and roll pinball bar and live music venue that has been a staple of downtown Lawrence since 1993. Originally a pinball arcade and burger joint, it has evolved into a premier destination for local and touring bands, featuring a sprawling, heated back patio and its own independent record label. (Lawrence Journal-World, The Pitch)
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Tuesday: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Wednesday: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Thursday: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Friday: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Saturday: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Sunday: 5:00 PM - 2:00 AM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (No cover) Monday – Wednesday 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Door cover varies for live music events and is CASH ONLY. (Facebook, Official Website)
highlights: Rotating collection of modern and classic pinball machines serviced by top-notch technicians (Official Website)
Large, heated outdoor patio and beer garden with a fireplace and warmers for year-round use (Tripadvisor, Yelp)
Regular live music schedule featuring early matinee shows and late-night performances (Official Website)
Home to Replay Records, an independent label releasing local and regional music (Official Website)
Classic dive bar atmosphere with a reputation for being the "hippest bar in town" (Tripadvisor)
Host to the Kaw Valley Kickball after-parties and other local community events (Official Website)
attractions.tips: The venue is strictly 21+ after 9:00 PM, though family-friendly matinee shows are occasionally held. Remember that door covers are cash-only, although the bar accepts both cash and cards with a $6.50 minimum. Check out the rotating pinball machines like Ghostbusters and AC/DC. (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: The Burger Stand at the Casbah, Watkins Museum of History, Eldridge Hotel, Lawrence Public Library, and various shops along Massachusetts Street. (Globalphile, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: South Park
attractions.description: South Park is Lawrence's oldest park, established in 1854 as part of the original town site. It serves as a central meeting place in downtown Lawrence, divided by Massachusetts Street into two distinct areas: the east side features formal gardens and historical structures, while the west side offers active recreation and playground facilities. City of Lawrence
attractions.hours: 6:00 a.m. – 11:30 p.m. daily City of Lawrence
highlights: William Kelly Bandstand (built in 1906) City of Lawrence
Theodore Roosevelt Fountain (dedicated by the President in 1910) City of Lawrence
Butterfly Garden and Monarch Watch habitat City of Lawrence
Lawrence City Band Summer Concert Series host City of Lawrence
Annual host for 'Art in the Park' Lawrence Art Guild
South Park Wading Pool for children City of Lawrence
attractions.tips: The park is divided by a busy street; the east side is best for quiet relaxation and gardens, while the west side is geared towards families with children. The wading pool is typically open from early June to early August. Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Watkins Museum of History, Liberty Memorial Central Middle School, Downtown Lawrence Historic District Wikipedia

attractions.name: Sunflower Outdoor & Bike Shop
attractions.category: Outdoor Gear and Bike Shop with Cafe & Pub
attractions.description: Sunflower Outdoor and Bike Shop is a premier cycling and outdoor outfitter in the nation, located in historic downtown Lawrence. Originally an Army-Navy surplus store established in 1972, it has grown into a community hub featuring a full retail shop, a café and pub, and a wellness studio.
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 10am-8pm; Saturday: 10am-6pm; Sunday: 12pm-5pm
highlights: Nationally recognized as one of the Best Bike Shops by the National Bicycle Dealers Association.
Features an on-site Café & Pub serving breakfast sandwiches, bison burgers, coffee, and cocktails.
Home to the Sunflower Wellness Studio, offering yoga, Pilates, and myofascial release classes.
Extensive selection of top brands like Patagonia, Kuhl, and Tarmac bikes.
Offers bike rentals and repair services for all levels of cyclists.
Hosts community rides, clinics, and educational events for outdoor enthusiasts.
attractions.tips: Visit the Sunflower Café & Pub for a meal or drink before or after shopping; check their event calendar for community bike rides and clinics. The wellness studio on the second floor offers drop-in yoga classes with great views of Massachusetts Street.
attractions.nearby: Watkins History Museum, Weaver's Department Store, The Dusty Bookshelf, South Park, Baker Wetlands

attractions.name: The Bottleneck
attractions.description: The Bottleneck is a legendary live music venue and bar located in the heart of historic downtown Lawrence, Kansas. Established in 1985, it has become a staple of the Midwest music scene, hosting a wide range of genres from indie rock and punk to bluegrass and hip-hop (Lawrence Chamber of Commerce, Travel Kansas). The venue is celebrated for its intimate, authentic atmosphere and has famously hosted early-career shows for iconic bands like Radiohead, Foo Fighters, and Nirvana (Kupi.com).
attractions.hours: Monday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Tuesday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Wednesday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Thursday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Friday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Saturday: 3:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Sunday: 7:30 PM - 2:00 AM (TripAdvisor, Kupi.com)
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event; typically $5 cover for local shows, with advance tickets for touring acts ranging from $18 to $70+ (The Bottleneck, SeatGeek).
highlights: Historical significance as a premier independent music venue in the Midwest since 1985 (Travel Kansas).
Famous alumni include early-career performances by Radiohead, Foo Fighters, and Nirvana (Kupi.com).
Intimate capacity of approximately 375, providing a close-up experience with performers (Kupi.com).
Versatile programming featuring indie, rock, punk, hip-hop, and local community events (Vivid Seats).
Located in the historic Lawrence Cultural District, just steps from the city's main dining and shopping on Massachusetts Street (Evendo).
attractions.tips: Arrive 30-60 minutes early for parking in nearby city lots or across the street; check the official website as hours often align with specific performance schedules; most shows are all-ages unless specified (Reddit, TicketSmarter).
attractions.nearby: Lawrence Farmers' Market, Lawrence Arts Center, Free State Brewing Co, Liberty Hall, Watkins Museum of History (TripAdvisor, Explore Lawrence).

attractions.name: The Dusty Bookshelf Lawrence
attractions.description: Located in historic downtown Lawrence, The Dusty Bookshelf is a beloved independently owned bookstore that has served the community for over 20 years. The shop is filled with a diverse collection of used and collectible books, offering cozy nooks and comfy chairs that invite readers to stay and explore. It is particularly well-known for its welcoming atmosphere, resident shop cats, and unique literary finds.

attractions.name: The Granada Theater
attractions.description: The Granada Theater is a historic live music venue and former movie palace located in the heart of Downtown Lawrence, Kansas. Originally built in 1928 as a vaudeville theater and later a cinema, it was repurposed in 1993 into a premier concert venue that hosts a wide range of national and international touring acts (Explore Lawrence, Wikipedia))
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday–Friday 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Saturday 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM. Performance times vary, with doors typically opening 60–90 minutes before showtime (LHS Budget, Vivid Seats)
attractions.days_open: Monday–Saturday (Box Office); Event days vary including Sundays (LHS Budget)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; typically $15–$50 for standard shows, with some events being Free. VIP tiers for certain artists can reach $145 (Official Website, LHS Budget)
highlights: Historic 1928 building designed by the renowned Boller Brothers (Wikipedia))
Transformed from a 1930s silent film theater to a top-tier live music host in 1993 (Lawrence Business Magazine)
Has hosted legendary musical acts including Weezer, Smashing Pumpkins, John Mayer, and The Killers (Hernly Architects)
Offers an intimate concert experience with a capacity of approximately 900 (Hernly Architects)
Centrally located on Lawrence's iconic Massachusetts Street near the University of Kansas (Wikipedia))
Features a state-of-the-art sound system and a spacious dance floor for high-energy performances (Avanzert)
attractions.tips: Arrive 30-60 minutes early to find parking in the busy downtown area; it is highly recommended to purchase tickets in advance as popular shows often sell out; most performances are all-ages unless specifically noted (Ticket Fairy, TicketSmarter)
attractions.nearby: Watkins Museum of History, Liberty Hall, South Park, Free State Brewing Co., and various shops and restaurants on Massachusetts Street (Globalphile, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: The Historic Eldridge Hotel
attractions.description: The Historic Eldridge Hotel is an integral part of Lawrence's history, originally founded in 1855 as the Free State Hotel by anti-slavery settlers (Official Site). After being destroyed multiple times during the Civil War era, it was rebuilt each time, eventually being restored to its 1925 grandeur in a 2005 renovation (Wikipedia). Today, it stands as a premier all-suite hotel on "the most historic corner in Kansas," offering a blend of modern hospitality and timeless charm (Official Site).
attractions.hours: Hotel: Open 24/7; Reservation Department: Mon–Fri 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM, Sat 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Eldridge House Grille: Brunch (Sat 8–11 AM, Sun 8 AM – 2 PM), Lunch (Mon 11 AM – 2 PM, Tue–Sat 11 AM – 4 PM), Dinner (Tue–Thu 4–9 PM, Fri–Sat 4–10 PM); Jayhawker Bar: Mon 11 AM – 3 PM, Tue–Fri 11 AM – Close, Sat 9 AM – Close, Sun 9 AM – 3 PM (Official Site, Official Site).
attractions.admission: Free for entry to the lobby and public areas; guest room rates typically range from $216 to $3,400+ depending on the season and events (Momondo, Tripadvisor).
highlights: Occupies the "most historic corner in Kansas" with roots dating back to the town\
haunted
Big 6
attractions.tips: Don't miss a visit to the basement to see the Big 6 room's historic mascots. For parking, metered street spaces are free after 6 PM and on Sundays; otherwise, use the Vermont Street Parking Garage. If you prefer a darker room, request one that doesn't face Massachusetts Street to avoid the bright downtown street lights (Official Site, Official Site, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Breakout Lawrence, Phoenix Gallery, Liberty Hall, Watkins Museum of History (Tripadvisor).

attractions.description: The Jackpot Saloon & Music Hall was a high-energy music venue and bar located in the heart of downtown Lawrence. Known for its intimate rock-and-roll atmosphere and cavernous layout, it hosted a diverse array of local, regional, and national touring acts across genres like indie rock, punk, and folk (Gigtown, Sonicbids).

attractions.name: The Raven Book Store
attractions.description: Founded in 1987, the Raven Book Store is a nationally recognized, employee-owned independent bookstore in downtown Lawrence. It specializes in mysteries, fiction, poetry, and social justice, while serving as a hub for community engagement and small business advocacy. The shop is also well-known for its resident bookstore cats, Sammy and Dean.
attractions.hours: Mon - Sat: 9:00am - 8:00pm; Sun: 12:00pm - 5:00pm
highlights: Employee-owned since 2022 and winner of Publishers Weekly's 2022 Bookstore of the Year.
Strong legacy as a mystery-specialty store with expanded collections in poetry and activism.
Active participant in the Paper Plains Literary Festival and local arts partnerships.
National reputation for pro-small business advocacy and anti-Amazon initiatives.
Features unique programs like the 'Book Mail' subscription and 'Surprise Fun Boxes.'
Home to two popular resident bookstore cats, Sammy and Dean.
attractions.tips: Check their event calendar for frequent author readings and community gatherings. Don't miss the 'Perfectly Queer Little Library' or the chance to visit with the store cats. If you need a gift, they offer wrapping services for a small fee.

attractions.name: Watkins Museum of History
attractions.description: The Watkins Museum of History is housed in the iconic 1888 Watkins Land Mortgage and National Bank building, a masterpiece of Richardsonian Romanesque architecture. The museum showcases the diverse heritage of Lawrence and Douglas County through three floors of exhibits, including significant coverage of "Bleeding Kansas," Quantrill's Raid, and the local Underground Railroad. Visitors can explore a wide range of artifacts, from a 1920s electric car to James Naismith's desk, while learning about the area's struggle for equality and its transformation from a frontier town. Watkins Museum of History, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sunday: Closed, Monday: Closed Watkins Museum of History
attractions.admission: Free (donations accepted) Watkins Museum of History, Roxie on the Road

Exhibits on "Bleeding Kansas" and the 1863 Quantrill\
attractions.tips: Admission is free to the public. Metered street parking is available in front of the museum and on 11th Street, while free two-hour parking is available at a public lot on Vermont Street. The museum is fully accessible via an elevator. For in-depth historical or genealogical research, visitors should book an appointment in advance for the Kramer Family Research Room. Watkins Museum of History, Watkins Museum of History

It specializes in hand-crafted, slow-fermented hearth breads baked in a wood-fired brick oven, and also offers breakfast and lunch options in a cafe lined with local artwork. attractions.name: Zen Zero
attractions.description: Zen Zero is an Asian casual dining restaurant located in the heart of historic downtown Lawrence, Kansas. Established in 2002, it offers a diverse menu featuring authentic flavors from Thailand, Nepal, Tibet, Vietnam, and Japan, served in a bustling and welcoming atmosphere. The restaurant is well-known for its fresh ingredients and extensive selection of vegetarian and gluten-free options. (Zen Zero, Yelp)

attractions.name: Allen Fieldhouse
attractions.category: University Basketball Arena, Museum, and Landmark
attractions.description: Allen Fieldhouse is a historic indoor arena on the University of Kansas campus, serving as the legendary home of the Jayhawks men's and women's basketball teams. Opened in 1955 and nicknamed "The Phog," it is widely regarded as one of college basketball's most prestigious venues, famous for its deafening crowd noise and rich athletic tradition. The facility also houses the Booth Family Hall of Athletics and the DeBruce Center, which displays the original rules of basketball. Wikipedia, Travel Kansas
attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. (Standard public hours); Saturday: Open for pre-purchased guided tours only; Sunday: Closed to the public. Hours are subject to change on gamedays and university holidays. University of Kansas Athletics
attractions.days_open: Monday – Saturday (Public hours Monday – Friday; Tours only on Saturday)
attractions.admission: Self-guided visits to the Booth Family Hall of Athletics and DeBruce Center are free; guided tours (The Jayhawk Experience) are approximately $25 per person. Game tickets vary by event and typically require advance purchase. University of Kansas Athletics, KU Admissions
highlights: James Naismith Court, named after the inventor of basketball who was also KU's first coach.
The Original Rules of 'Basket Ball' written by James Naismith, housed in the attached DeBruce Center.
Booth Family Hall of Athletics, a 19,000-square-foot museum celebrating KU's athletic history.
Guinness World Record for the loudest crowd roar in an indoor sports arena (130.4 dB).
The 'Pay Heed, All Who Enter Here' banner and retired jerseys of legends like Wilt Chamberlain.
Legendary gameday traditions including the 'Rock Chalk, Jayhawk' chant.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to explore the Hall of Athletics and the original rules before the gameday rush. Be prepared for extreme noise levels during games; earplugs are recommended for those sensitive to sound. Parking near the arena is heavily restricted on gamedays and usually requires a permit, so check campus parking maps in advance. Airial Travel, University of Kansas Athletics
attractions.nearby: DeBruce Center, Massachusetts Street (Downtown Lawrence), Potter Lake, The Campanile, Kansas Memorial Union. Reddit, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Kansas State Capitol (Kansas Statehouse)
attractions.description: The Kansas State Capitol, also known as the Kansas Statehouse, is the state's most important architectural treasure and the seat of its executive and legislative branches. Built over 37 years starting in 1866, the French Renaissance-style building features a copper dome topped by the 'Ad Astra' statue and is renowned for its magnificent murals, including works by John Steuart Curry. Kansas Historical Society, Wikipedia
attractions.address: SW 8th and SW Van Buren, Topeka, KS 66612 (Visitor Entrance) or 300 SW 10th Avenue (Official)

Free underground parking is available on 8th Avenue between Harrison and Van Buren. If you plan to take the Dome Tour, be prepared for a 296-step climb through narrow and steep stairs. Kansas Historical Society, Tripadvisor

attractions.description: The Konza Prairie Biological Station is a 3,487-hectare native tallgrass prairie preserve in the Flint Hills of northeastern Kansas. It is jointly owned by The Nature Conservancy and Kansas State University, serving as a field research station dedicated to ecological research, education, and conservation. (KPBS Website, Wikipedia)

Bison herd of approximately 200 animals on 2,400 acres (Wikipedia)
UNESCO Biosphere Reserve (formerly designated) (Wikipedia)
Kings Creek crossings and lowland gallery forests (KEEP Website)
Scenic overlooks of the Flint Hills region (KEEP Website)
attractions.tips: No dogs or bicycles are allowed on the trails; stay on designated paths to avoid impacting research. Bring your own water and snacks, as there are no trash cans or picnic areas on the trails. Wear comfortable, closed-toe shoes for hiking rough terrain. (KEEP Website)

attractions.name: Mount Oread
attractions.description: Mount Oread, known informally as 'The Hill,' is the historic limestone ridge that serves as the main campus for the University of Kansas. It offers panoramic views of the Kansas and Wakarusa River valleys and holds deep historical significance as a site for early abolitionist settlers and a lookout point during the 1863 Quantrill's Raid. Today, it is a vibrant center for academics, athletics, and culture, featuring historic architecture and renowned museums.
attractions.hours: Grounds: Open 24/7; Jayhawk Welcome Center: Monday–Friday 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (KU Alumni Association)
attractions.admission: Free for campus grounds and self-guided tours (University of Kansas Admissions); suggested $7 donation for Natural History Museum (One Delightful Life); ticketed guided tours available (KU Athletics)
highlights: Jayhawk Boulevard: The scenic central artery of campus lined with historic limestone buildings and red-tiled roofs (Explore Lawrence).
The Campanile: A 120-foot carillon bell tower and World War II memorial that is a central campus landmark (Explore Lawrence).
Allen Fieldhouse & Booth Hall of Athletics: One of college basketball's most storied venues, featuring exhibits on the history of the sport (KU Athletics).
DeBruce Center: The permanent home of James Naismith’s original 'Rules of Basket Ball' (One Delightful Life).
KU Natural History Museum: Located in historic Dyche Hall, it features four floors of exhibits including the famous Panorama of North American Wildlife (Moving Proz).
Spencer Museum of Art: Houses a collection of nearly 36,000 artworks ranging from ancient Egypt to contemporary pieces (Globalphile).
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the campus terrain is steep. For the best views of the skyline and Memorial Stadium, visit the rooftop of the Oread Hotel. Start your visit at the Jayhawk Welcome Center to pick up a campus map and view interactive exhibits (One Delightful Life, University of Kansas Admissions).

(National Park Service, The Nature Conservancy)
